name: Panel (Python Dashboards)

auth.notes: Local library; basic_auth available for deployment; OAuth integration for cloud

performance.notes: WebSocket-based reactive updates; Bokeh server backend; scales with Panel Serve

agent_readiness.notes: Python dashboarding framework from HoloViz. Wraps any Python visualization (matplotlib, Bokeh, Plotly, Vega, hvPlot, Datashader, Folium, Deck.gl) in a reactive component model. Widgets (sliders, dropdowns, buttons), layouts, tabs, accordions. Async support, streaming data, chat interfaces. Deploy with `panel serve`. Works in Jupyter, scripts, and web apps. More Pythonic than Dash; better viz library integration than Streamlit.

gotchas: Bokeh server required for interactive deployment (not just static HTML)
More complex deployment than Streamlit for simple use cases
Documentation fragmented across multiple HoloViz projects
Reactive programming model has learning curve
best_when: Complex dashboards needing multiple viz library types
Data science teams wanting maximum Python flexibility
Streaming data dashboards with real-time updates
avoid_when: Simple dashboards (Streamlit or Gradio are easier)
Production web apps requiring full frontend control (use React + FastAPI)
Teams without Python data science background
summary: Panel is HoloViz's Python dashboarding framework. Wraps any Python visualization library (Bokeh, Plotly, Matplotlib, hvPlot, Datashader, Folium) in a reactive component model. Widgets, layouts, tabs, async streaming, chat interfaces. Deploy with `panel serve`. Works in Jupyter and scripts. More flexible than Streamlit with better visualization library integration. Best when already using HoloViz ecosystem tools.
